value.Title: Mines Names New Provost, CEE Department Head

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH1-BNM3-RXV5-S2NW-00000-00Mines Names New Provost, CEE Department Head2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:42ZLexisNexisMines Names New Provost, CEE Department HeadTargeted News ServiceRAPID CITY, South Dakota

The South Dakota School of Mines and Technology issued the following news release:

* * *

Mines Names New Provost, CEE Department Head

*

Two respected leaders at South Dakota Mines are stepping into new roles this spring.

Jim Stone, Ph.D., current department head of civil and environmental engineering (CEE), has been appointed the university's next provost and vice president for academic and student affairs. He succeeds Lance Roberts, Ph.D., who is stepping down to pursue opportunities outside higher education. Stone will begin his new role on May 22, 2026.

Stone previously served as interim provost during the 2025 university presidential search, a period when Roberts was serving as interim president.

In addition, Marc Robinson, Ph.D., professor and associate CEE department head, will step into the role of department head.

"Jim Stone and Marc Robinson are both highly respected leaders who have made lasting contributions to South Dakota Mines through their dedication to students, research and the success of their department," said Mines President Brian Tande, Ph.D. "I look forward to working with both of them in their new roles."

Stone has been a fixture at South Dakota Mines for more than 20 years. He earned undergraduate and master's degrees at Virginia Tech before working for five years in industry, designing environmental remediation systems. Stone joined the Mines faculty in 2003, following the completion of his doctorate at Penn State University in 2002.

"Dr. Stone brings a thoughtful, collaborative approach and deep understanding of our institution to his new role as provost," Tande said. "I am confident he will help guide Mines forward as we prepare students to meet the complex challenges of the future."

As a professor, Stone's research focused on a range of collaborative, interdisciplinary projects on sustainability and the environment. His work delved into issues around agriculture and environment, uranium mining, water quality and bioenergy production. Stone worked with environmental-focused students on relevant, real-world case studies and assignments to better prepare them to handle tomorrow's complex environmental problems.

He has been CEE department head since 2020.

"It's an honor and privilege to be considered for this leadership position at South Dakota Mines," Stone said. "I'm looking forward to working with our talented faculty and staff across campus to help train our current and future science and engineering students for the grand challenges that await them in their future careers."

In his free time, Stone enjoys everything outdoors - hiking, biking, snowboarding, camping. He recently completed the Dakota 5-0 mountain bike race, a challenging singletrack race in Spearfish, with his son Zander, a freshman civil and environmental engineering major at Mines.

Robinson joined the Mines faculty in 2009. He earned his undergraduate and master's degrees from Utah State University. He worked at ABS Consulting, where he focused on the design of new buildings and the seismic retrofitting of existing structures. In 2008, he earned his doctorate in structural engineering from the University of California, San Diego.

"Dr. Robinson's leadership, industry experience and commitment to excellence make him exceptionally well-suited to lead the civil and environmental engineering department," Tande said.

Robinson has advised numerous undergraduate and graduate students in a variety of research areas during his nearly two decades at Mines.

"I have been part of the CEE department at South Dakota Mines for 17 years, and I look forward to coming to work every day because of our outstanding students, faculty and staff," Robinson said. "I'm excited to build on the strong foundation already in place as we continue to support our CEE family in achieving their professional goals."

***

urn:contentItem:6JHC-MNG3-RT9K-655T-00000-00MILBANK LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYZES CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS

In a recent webinar, Milbank attorneys examined the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets, which was released last week. The event, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' took place on Tuesday. This follows the CFTC's announcement on 17th March 2026, introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, initiating a 45-day public comment period that concludes on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened the discussion by highlighting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, which surged from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026. This growth has attracted attention not only from those aiming to rejuvenate financial markets but also from the courts and state regulators. The session featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who provided insights on the current state of the prediction market industry and the implications of the CFTC's recent announcements for market operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, which he described as a significant clarification. He stated, 'The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses.' This recognition could influence ongoing litigation regarding the classification of such contracts.Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He remarked on the extensive list of questions posed for public comment, which invites input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination. 'It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things,' he added, indicating a focus on public interest.To navigate the changing regulatory landscape, Milbank attorneys outlined practical steps for prediction market operators. Recommendations included evaluating existing contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland also provided an overview of over 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, indicating that the legal landscape remains complex and uncertain, especially regarding the question of whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO FACILITATE DISCUSSION FOR KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDERS

Milbank LLP will host a call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is set to take place today, 5th March 2026, at 3:30 p.m. (EST), addressing concerns raised by numerous bondholders regarding the recent Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.This call is particularly significant as Milbank has received indications of interest from holders of a substantial portion of the Notes, suggesting a heightened level of engagement among investors. Bondholders who wish to participate in the call must provide their detailed contact information, along with their holdings information on a series-by-series basis, to ensure their inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded over 150 years ago in New York, specializes in providing innovative legal services to clients globally. With offices in key financial hubs including London, Hong Kong, and S o Paulo, Milbank collaborates across various practices to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments, in achieving their strategic objectives.In addition to hosting calls for Kennedy-Wilson bondholders, Milbank has been actively analyzing recent developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A. This highlights the firm’s commitment to providing timely and relevant legal advice to its clients in an ever-evolving financial landscape.As Milbank continues to expand its footprint and enhance its services, the firm remains dedicated to fostering transparent communication between issuers and investors, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District of New York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON FINANCE TEAM WITH NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has announced the addition of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office. This strategic move underscores the firm’s commitment to bolstering its private capital capabilities in the UK and European markets. The announcement follows the date of 21st January 2026, marking a significant step in the firm’s expansion plans.Christopher McLaughlin will spearhead the firm's private equity sponsor and borrower acquisition, as well as leveraged finance practices in London. Meanwhile, Alastair McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending. Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, expressed enthusiasm for the new hires, noting that their expertise will enhance the firm's ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading figure in acquisition and leveraged finance law within Europe, boasting extensive experience in advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. His accolades include being ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. Similarly, McVeigh is well-respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, positioning him as a valuable asset to Sullivan & Cromwell’s growing practice.The co-managing partners of Sullivan & Cromwell's London office, Kon Asimacopoulos and John Horsfield-Bradbury, highlighted that the arrivals of McLaughlin and McVeigh are integral to the execution of the firm's private capital strategy. They emphasized that the new partners’ top-tier expertise aligns seamlessly with the firm’s global platform and its established finance practice in the U.S.Sullivan & Cromwell's Private Equity Practice is known for representing top financial sponsors and private capital investors in complex transactions globally. With an average deal size of $2.3 billion for completed European private equity transactions in 2024, according to Mergermarket, the firm’s reputation for excellence continues to grow. The addition of McLaughlin and McVeigh is expected to further enhance the firm’s capabilities across the spectrum of private credit and leveraged finance, solidifying its position as a leader in these sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FRESHFIELDS EXPANDS US PRACTICE WITH WILLKIE'S MARY EATON

In a strategic move to enhance its corporate litigation capabilities, Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer has successfully recruited Mary Eaton from Willkie Farr & Gallagher. This development comes on the heels of Freshfields' expansion efforts in the US market, including the recent hiring of a team of prominent corporate lawyers from Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton. Eaton, who previously led the corporate litigation practice at Willkie, will now co-head Freshfields' securities and shareholder litigation department alongside her former Cleary colleague, Meredith Kotler.The date of this announcement is 25th April 2026. Freshfields' proactive recruitment strategy reflects its ambition to solidify its presence in the competitive US legal landscape. The firm is already recognized for its corporate litigation expertise, as evidenced by its recent financial performance, which saw a 1% increase in profits to £688 million (approximately $860 million) last year. By attracting seasoned litigators with extensive networks, Freshfields aims to bolster its reputation in the corporate litigation domain.Eaton expressed enthusiasm about her new role, highlighting the opportunity to establish a practice from the ground up with two senior women litigators at the forefront. Her extensive experience includes representing high-profile clients such as CBS, Facebook, and Merck & Co. in complex shareholder litigation matters. This appointment comes at a time when shareholder litigation is on the rise, a trend that is expected to continue as the market stabilizes post-pandemic.Ethan Klingsberg, who has been pivotal in Freshfields' recent expansion, emphasized that Eaton and Kotler bring a powerful combination of expertise and experience, particularly in advising clients on litigation risks and managing corporate disputes. Their leadership is tailored to meet the evolving demands of Freshfields' clients in an increasingly litigious environment.Despite some challenges, including the recent departure of former M&A head Mitchell Presser to Morrison & Foerster, Freshfields remains committed to building its corporate legal team. With Eaton's addition, the firm is well-positioned to tackle significant shareholder litigation disputes in the future, as it seeks to capture a larger share of the M&A market once it rebounds.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER SECURES €105M FOR MUTARES' US EXPANSION

In a strategic move to bolster its presence in the United States, Mutares SE & Co. KGaA has successfully raised €105 million, with legal guidance from Willkie Farr & Gallagher. This financing marks a significant step for Mutares as it seeks to expand its operations in the lucrative US market.On 22nd April 2026, Mutares announced the successful completion of its fundraising round, a critical milestone that underscores the company's commitment to growth and diversification. The funds will be primarily allocated towards acquisitions and investments in key sectors, enhancing Mutares' portfolio and operational footprint in North America.Willkie Farr & Gallagher played a pivotal role in structuring the financial framework and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements throughout the fundraising process. Their expertise in mergers and acquisitions has been instrumental in bridging the gap between Mutares' strategic objectives and the complexities of the US market landscape.The expansion into the US is part of Mutares' broader strategy to capitalize on emerging opportunities in various sectors, including technology and manufacturing. With this injection of capital, the company is poised to explore potential acquisitions that align with its long-term vision of fostering sustainable growth and operational excellence.As Mutares embarks on this ambitious journey, the support from Willkie Farr & Gallagher not only highlights the law firm’s capability in navigating complex financial transactions but also positions Mutares as a formidable player in the US market. Investors will be watching closely to see how this expansion unfolds and what further opportunities may arise for both Mutares and its legal advisers.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER ACHIEVES CERTIFICATION UNDER INTERNATIONAL AI STANDARDS

Willkie Farr & Gallagher has become the latest law firm to receive certification under international artificial intelligence standards, marking a significant milestone in the legal sector's embrace of cutting-edge technology. This achievement reflects the firm's commitment to integrating AI responsibly and effectively into its practice, as it navigates complex legal environments shaped by technological advancements. The certification was awarded on 14th April 2026.In an era where AI is increasingly influencing various industries, Willkie Farr & Gallagher's certification positions it as a frontrunner among legal firms adapting to these changes. The firm is now recognized for adhering to globally accepted AI principles, which emphasize transparency, accountability, and ethical use of technology in legal processes. This move is expected to bolster client confidence, as clients increasingly seek firms that prioritize ethical standards in their use of AI.The certification process involved rigorous assessments and compliance checks, ensuring that Willkie Farr & Gallagher meets the highest standards in AI application. The firm has implemented robust protocols to manage AI tools effectively, which not only enhances operational efficiency but also mitigates risks associated with AI deployment. As legal practices evolve, this certification could serve as a competitive advantage for Willkie Farr & Gallagher in attracting tech-savvy clients.Industry experts believe that Willkie Farr & Gallagher's initiative may set a precedent for other law firms considering similar certifications. As the legal landscape becomes more intertwined with technology, the necessity for regulations and standards becomes paramount. This certification could encourage a broader movement within the legal profession to adopt AI responsibly, ensuring that firms maintain ethical boundaries while leveraging technological innovations.As Willkie Farr & Gallagher continues to integrate AI into its operations, the firm is expected to lead discussions on best practices and ethical considerations surrounding AI in law. This certification not only enhances the firm's reputation but also positions it as a thought leader in the ongoing dialogue about technology's role in the legal field. The firm is set to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of legal services as it navigates this complex landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB STRENGTHENS DIGITAL ASSETS GROUP WITH IGOR KLEYMAN

Cleary Gottlieb announced the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, focusing on digital assets, artificial intelligence (AI), and fintech. On 20th November 2025, the New York-based firm communicated to its associates via an internal memo that annual bonuses will range from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior associates, according to information reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath plans to offer special bonuses ranging from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring the payouts recently made by rival firm Milbank. This strategic move reinforces Cravath’s position as a traditional first mover in the competitive landscape of associate compensation within the legal sector. While the firm declined to provide further comments, the bonus structure aligns with expectations set by previous years.Following Cravath's announcement, other major firms are already responding. Paul Hastings, for instance, quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which reflect a similar scale to those set by Cravath. A spokesperson from Paul Hastings also chose not to comment, indicating that the firm is likely following the trend established by Cravath.The bonuses announced by Cravath Swaine & Moore are consistent with those distributed by large U.S. law firms last year, as many associates now earn annual salaries ranging from $225,000 to $435,000 depending on their class year. The competitive nature of the legal profession often leads firms to match salary and bonus figures to retain top talent, and Cravath’s decision is expected to influence other firms in the sector.Looking ahead, U.S. law firms are poised for a profitable 2025, with large and midsized firms experiencing a significant increase in client demand, particularly in transactional practices. This positive outlook was highlighted in a recent analysis of firm financial data, suggesting that the legal industry is gearing up for another successful year, further emphasizing the importance of competitive compensation packages like those offered by Cravath.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WESTWARD WITH SKADDEN PARTNER JASON RUSSELL

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to expand its footprint by opening a new office in Los Angeles, recruiting Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom’s LA office. This strategic move is expected to enhance Davis Polk's capabilities in a city known for its vibrant legal landscape, particularly in the sectors of sports and entertainment. The firm plans to officially open its Los Angeles office on 14th April 2026.Russell, a distinguished litigator with years of experience at Skadden, has represented prominent clients such as the National Basketball Association, Christie’s Inc., MGA Entertainment Inc., and Credit Acceptance Corp. His expertise in high-stakes litigation and complex class actions positions Davis Polk favorably in the competitive Los Angeles market. Additionally, Russell has served on Skadden’s policy committee, underscoring his influence within the firm.The opening of the Los Angeles office is a significant milestone for Davis Polk, which has been gaining prominence in the legal sector. The firm had a remarkable year in 2025, achieving a 26% increase in revenue to $3.2 billion, which was the highest growth among major law firms. In comparison, Skadden recently crossed the $4 billion revenue threshold but did not match Davis Polk's growth rate. This success has translated into attractive partner profits, with Davis Polk partners earning an average of $9.8 million.Davis Polk is not alone in recognizing the potential of the Los Angeles market. The city has seen a surge of interest from leading law firms, including Willkie Farr & Gallagher, which established an office in 2021 and has since expanded to over 100 attorneys. Moreover, Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton & Garrison recently opened an office in LA following a strategic acquisition from Kirkland & Ellis, indicating strong competition for top legal talent and clients.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles debut, further hiring plans are already underway. With Jason Russell at the helm and an ambitious growth strategy, the firm aims to carve out a significant presence in the region. The legal industry will be closely watching how this expansion unfolds in a city that has long been a hub for major law firms.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYZES CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS

In a recent webinar, Milbank attorneys examined the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets, which was released last week. The event, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' took place on Tuesday. This follows the CFTC's announcement on 17th March 2026, introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, initiating a 45-day public comment period that concludes on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened the discussion by highlighting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, which surged from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026. This growth has attracted attention not only from those aiming to rejuvenate financial markets but also from the courts and state regulators. The session featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who provided insights on the current state of the prediction market industry and the implications of the CFTC's recent announcements for market operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, which he described as a significant clarification. He stated, 'The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses.' This recognition could influence ongoing litigation regarding the classification of such contracts.Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He remarked on the extensive list of questions posed for public comment, which invites input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination. 'It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things,' he added, indicating a focus on public interest.To navigate the changing regulatory landscape, Milbank attorneys outlined practical steps for prediction market operators. Recommendations included evaluating existing contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland also provided an overview of over 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, indicating that the legal landscape remains complex and uncertain, especially regarding the question of whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO FACILITATE DISCUSSION FOR KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDERS

Milbank LLP will host a call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is set to take place today, 5th March 2026, at 3:30 p.m. (EST), addressing concerns raised by numerous bondholders regarding the recent Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.This call is particularly significant as Milbank has received indications of interest from holders of a substantial portion of the Notes, suggesting a heightened level of engagement among investors. Bondholders who wish to participate in the call must provide their detailed contact information, along with their holdings information on a series-by-series basis, to ensure their inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded over 150 years ago in New York, specializes in providing innovative legal services to clients globally. With offices in key financial hubs including London, Hong Kong, and S o Paulo, Milbank collaborates across various practices to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments, in achieving their strategic objectives.In addition to hosting calls for Kennedy-Wilson bondholders, Milbank has been actively analyzing recent developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A. This highlights the firm’s commitment to providing timely and relevant legal advice to its clients in an ever-evolving financial landscape.As Milbank continues to expand its footprint and enhance its services, the firm remains dedicated to fostering transparent communication between issuers and investors, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON FINANCE TEAM WITH NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has announced the addition of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office. This strategic move underscores the firm’s commitment to bolstering its private capital capabilities in the UK and European markets. The announcement follows the date of 21st January 2026, marking a significant step in the firm’s expansion plans.Christopher McLaughlin will spearhead the firm's private equity sponsor and borrower acquisition, as well as leveraged finance practices in London. Meanwhile, Alastair McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending. Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, expressed enthusiasm for the new hires, noting that their expertise will enhance the firm's ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading figure in acquisition and leveraged finance law within Europe, boasting extensive experience in advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. His accolades include being ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. Similarly, McVeigh is well-respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, positioning him as a valuable asset to Sullivan & Cromwell’s growing practice.The co-managing partners of Sullivan & Cromwell's London office, Kon Asimacopoulos and John Horsfield-Bradbury, highlighted that the arrivals of McLaughlin and McVeigh are integral to the execution of the firm's private capital strategy. They emphasized that the new partners’ top-tier expertise aligns seamlessly with the firm’s global platform and its established finance practice in the U.S.Sullivan & Cromwell's Private Equity Practice is known for representing top financial sponsors and private capital investors in complex transactions globally. With an average deal size of $2.3 billion for completed European private equity transactions in 2024, according to Mergermarket, the firm’s reputation for excellence continues to grow. The addition of McLaughlin and McVeigh is expected to further enhance the firm’s capabilities across the spectrum of private credit and leveraged finance, solidifying its position as a leader in these sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FRESHFIELDS EXPANDS US PRACTICE WITH WILLKIE'S MARY EATON

In a strategic move to enhance its corporate litigation capabilities, Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer has successfully recruited Mary Eaton from Willkie Farr & Gallagher. This development comes on the heels of Freshfields' expansion efforts in the US market, including the recent hiring of a team of prominent corporate lawyers from Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton. Eaton, who previously led the corporate litigation practice at Willkie, will now co-head Freshfields' securities and shareholder litigation department alongside her former Cleary colleague, Meredith Kotler.The date of this announcement is 25th April 2026. Freshfields' proactive recruitment strategy reflects its ambition to solidify its presence in the competitive US legal landscape. The firm is already recognized for its corporate litigation expertise, as evidenced by its recent financial performance, which saw a 1% increase in profits to £688 million (approximately $860 million) last year. By attracting seasoned litigators with extensive networks, Freshfields aims to bolster its reputation in the corporate litigation domain.Eaton expressed enthusiasm about her new role, highlighting the opportunity to establish a practice from the ground up with two senior women litigators at the forefront. Her extensive experience includes representing high-profile clients such as CBS, Facebook, and Merck & Co. in complex shareholder litigation matters. This appointment comes at a time when shareholder litigation is on the rise, a trend that is expected to continue as the market stabilizes post-pandemic.Ethan Klingsberg, who has been pivotal in Freshfields' recent expansion, emphasized that Eaton and Kotler bring a powerful combination of expertise and experience, particularly in advising clients on litigation risks and managing corporate disputes. Their leadership is tailored to meet the evolving demands of Freshfields' clients in an increasingly litigious environment.Despite some challenges, including the recent departure of former M&A head Mitchell Presser to Morrison & Foerster, Freshfields remains committed to building its corporate legal team. With Eaton's addition, the firm is well-positioned to tackle significant shareholder litigation disputes in the future, as it seeks to capture a larger share of the M&A market once it rebounds.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER SECURES €105M FOR MUTARES' US EXPANSION

In a strategic move to bolster its presence in the United States, Mutares SE & Co.KGaA has successfully raised €105 million, with legal guidance from Willkie Farr & Gallagher. This financing marks a significant step for Mutares as it seeks to expand its operations in the lucrative US market.On 22nd April 2026, Mutares announced the successful completion of its fundraising round, a critical milestone that underscores the company's commitment to growth and diversification. The funds will be primarily allocated towards acquisitions and investments in key sectors, enhancing Mutares' portfolio and operational footprint in North America.Willkie Farr & Gallagher played a pivotal role in structuring the financial framework and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements throughout the fundraising process. Their expertise in mergers and acquisitions has been instrumental in bridging the gap between Mutares' strategic objectives and the complexities of the US market landscape.The expansion into the US is part of Mutares' broader strategy to capitalize on emerging opportunities in various sectors, including technology and manufacturing. With this injection of capital, the company is poised to explore potential acquisitions that align with its long-term vision of fostering sustainable growth and operational excellence.As Mutares embarks on this ambitious journey, the support from Willkie Farr & Gallagher not only highlights the law firm’s capability in navigating complex financial transactions but also positions Mutares as a formidable player in the US market. Investors will be watching closely to see how this expansion unfolds and what further opportunities may arise for both Mutares and its legal advisers.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER ACHIEVES CERTIFICATION UNDER INTERNATIONAL AI STANDARDS

Willkie Farr & Gallagher has become the latest law firm to receive certification under international artificial intelligence standards, marking a significant milestone in the legal sector's embrace of cutting-edge technology. This achievement reflects the firm's commitment to integrating AI responsibly and effectively into its practice, as it navigates complex legal environments shaped by technological advancements. The certification was awarded on 14th April 2026.In an era where AI is increasingly influencing various industries, Willkie Farr & Gallagher's certification positions it as a frontrunner among legal firms adapting to these changes. The firm is now recognized for adhering to globally accepted AI principles, which emphasize transparency, accountability, and ethical use of technology in legal processes. This move is expected to bolster client confidence, as clients increasingly seek firms that prioritize ethical standards in their use of AI.The certification process involved rigorous assessments and compliance checks, ensuring that Willkie Farr & Gallagher meets the highest standards in AI application. The firm has implemented robust protocols to manage AI tools effectively, which not only enhances operational efficiency but also mitigates risks associated with AI deployment. As legal practices evolve, this certification could serve as a competitive advantage for Willkie Farr & Gallagher in attracting tech-savvy clients.Industry experts believe that Willkie Farr & Gallagher's initiative may set a precedent for other law firms considering similar certifications. As the legal landscape becomes more intertwined with technology, the necessity for regulations and standards becomes paramount. This certification could encourage a broader movement within the legal profession to adopt AI responsibly, ensuring that firms maintain ethical boundaries while leveraging technological innovations.As Willkie Farr & Gallagher continues to integrate AI into its operations, the firm is expected to lead discussions on best practices and ethical considerations surrounding AI in law. This certification not only enhances the firm's reputation but also positions it as a thought leader in the ongoing dialogue about technology's role in the legal field. The firm is set to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of legal services as it navigates this complex landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB STRENGTHENS DIGITAL ASSETS GROUP WITH IGOR KLEYMAN

Cleary Gottlieb announced the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, focusing on digital assets, artificial intelligence (AI), and fintech. This announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in rapidly evolving sectors.Igor Kleyman rejoins Cleary Gottlieb after nearly a decade of experience advising clients on critical issues surrounding digital assets and AI. Most recently, he served as Head of Responsible AI Governance at Amazon, and prior to that, he held key legal positions at Nasdaq and Gemini Trust Company, where he navigated regulatory challenges and contributed to the development of pioneering financial products.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf expressed enthusiasm about Igor's return, stating, "Igor’s fluency across the transformational fintech and AI sectors, combined with his sophisticated understanding of financial regulatory structures and institutions, enhances our powerhouse of innovation in financial services." This sentiment underscores the firm's strategy to remain at the forefront of legal services in these dynamic fields.Igor will be part of Cleary Gottlieb's multidisciplinary digital assets group, which was recognized as a 2025 Law360 Practice Group of the Year. The group is noted for its efforts in shaping regulatory frameworks for fintech and managing significant enforcement actions for its digital asset clients. Igor's expertise in compliance and governance has played a crucial role in establishing industry standards.In his own words, Igor expressed excitement about his return to Cleary Gottlieb, stating, "I look forward to collaborating across Cleary’s global network to further strengthen our relationships with fintech companies and businesses exploring digital assets, tokenization, and AI offerings." This collaboration aligns with Cleary Gottlieb's longstanding reputation as a leader in digital assets and fintech law.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB WELCOMES BACK DIGITAL ASSETS AND AI SPECIALIST FROM AMAZON

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the return of a prominent expert in digital assets and artificial intelligence (AI) who previously held a senior position at Amazon. This strategic move reflects the firm's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the rapidly evolving digital landscape. The professional, whose return is eagerly anticipated within the legal community, had been instrumental in Amazon's initiatives related to AI technologies and digital asset management.On 20th April 2026, the firm confirmed that the digital assets and AI specialist has rejoined its ranks following a successful tenure at Amazon. This hire comes at a time when legal frameworks surrounding digital currencies and AI applications are becoming increasingly complex, necessitating experienced legal guidance. Cleary Gottlieb aims to leverage this expertise to better serve its clients, particularly in financial technology and regulatory compliance.The rejoining of this expert is a notable addition to Cleary Gottlieb's already robust practice in digital assets. The firm has been actively involved in advising clients on blockchain technologies, cryptocurrency regulations, and the implications of AI within the financial sector. By reintegrating this professional, Cleary Gottlieb not only strengthens its position in these specialized areas but also enhances its overall service offerings to clients navigating the intricacies of digital innovation.As the demand for legal services in the tech sector grows, firms like Cleary Gottlieb are increasingly focused on recruiting talent with unique expertise in emerging technologies. The legal landscape is witnessing a significant shift, with traditional practices evolving to meet the needs of clients in various industries, from fintech startups to established corporations looking to innovate. The return of this digital assets and AI expert signals Cleary Gottlieb's proactive approach to stay ahead in this competitive environment.With the legal implications of digital assets and AI continuing to unfold, the expertise of this newly reinstated professional is likely to be a significant asset for Cleary Gottlieb. Clients seeking guidance on navigating the regulatory complexities associated with these technologies will benefit from the firm's enhanced knowledge base. As Cleary Gottlieb positions itself at the forefront of the digital transformation, this hire may also spark increased interest and investment from clients eager to explore new opportunities in the digital economy.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB UNVEILS CX+, AN AI SOLUTION FOR LEGAL WORKFLOW OPTIMIZATION

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the launch of CX+, a proprietary AI-powered platform designed to enhance legal workflows through advanced contract intelligence and due diligence capabilities. The announcement was made on the 15th of April 2026, and CX+ is comprised of two main products: CX+Insights and CX+Transact, which cater specifically to the operational and transactional needs of in-house legal teams and deal professionals.Developed in collaboration with senior in-house clients and legal operations experts, CX+ was crafted to address specific challenges faced by legal teams. Carla Swansburg, CEO of ClearyX, stated, "We spent years speaking with clients about the problems that they wish technology would solve for them, and that generic tools simply aren't solving." The platform aims to provide tailored solutions that fit seamlessly within the existing technology stacks of clients, enhancing their operational efficiency.CX+ is designed to be adaptable and model-agnostic, allowing clients to configure the platform according to their unique workflows. The software prioritizes security, featuring secure code and rigorous testing for both accuracy and stability. Notably, CX+ can integrate with existing document management systems through custom APIs, enabling immediate value realization from the outset. Clients can choose to license CX+ as a standalone tool or combine it with additional services from ClearyX's expert team.CX+Insights specifically targets in-house legal teams seeking to derive systematic intelligence from their contract portfolios. The platform facilitates the loading of contracts, enabling users to query documents with AI-driven question bundles and generate actionable insights via custom dashboards and visualizations. As new documents are added, CX+Insights automatically updates its analytics, creating a continuous intelligence layer that enhances decision-making processes.CX+Transact, on the other hand, is tailored for contract review and M&A due diligence. It features a comprehensive review system that allows for tagging, filtering, and in-place editing, along with customizable workflows and real-time visibility into review progress. ClearyX has documented substantial cost and time savings of 40-60% on due diligence tasks compared to traditional manual review methods across more than 150 deals. This innovative platform underscores Cleary Gottlieb's commitment to leveraging technology to deliver exceptional client service.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB LEAD AI BATTERY FIRM IN $250M SPAC DEAL

By bringing onboard Russell, a seasoned litigator with a robust portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie's, and MGA Entertainment, the firm aims to solidify its footing in the lucrative Los Angeles legal landscape.Jason Russell, who has also served on Skadden's policy committee, is expected to play a pivotal role in establishing Davis Polk's presence in LA. His leadership is anticipated to attract additional talent as the firm plans to make further hires for the new office, enhancing its capabilities in the region.Davis Polk's move into Los Angeles not only showcases its ambition to broaden its geographic footprint but also highlights its strategy to compete with other major law firms in California. The firm's focus on high-profile cases and clients could translate into significant opportunities as it seeks to capitalize on the vibrant legal market.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles office's opening, the legal community will be watching closely. The firm's ability to leverage Russell's extensive experience and connections may position it favorably among clients seeking top-tier legal representation in one of the nation’s most competitive markets.
